textDisplay:
Spoiler Alert
















This one was improvement from previous one indeed I just didn't like abandoning plotlines such as why during 2nd film Newt seems to passive aggressively hate his brother while Theseus had his back, no emotional consequence of Leta's death on Theseus or Newt, Nagini just disappeared in wind I guess, Events that was such a big deal during last movie like Queenie's turn to dark side and Credence bloodline reveal was solved super easily barely an inconvenience Queenie doesn't even need Tina her own sister for coming back to her senses. And I still feel giving Jacob wand and Kama infiltration mission was nothing but increasing movie's runtime and why was Jacob needed for this team made for taking down Grindelwald if it was just for persuading Queenie wouldn't Tina be a better option because, I don't know, she is an ACTUAL WIT CH. Overall these fantastic beasts film sequels are like soft reboots of the previous film and it is a bit of stretch but it feels like Star wars sequel trilogy all over again it doesn't feel like they planned it at all and just made it up as it goes along it doesn't have a story to tell just cashing on nostalgia. Jacob and Lally was the MVP of this movie and everyone else couldn't keep me from being bored despite actors doing great performance its just the material they were given.

textDisplay: This movie was truly sad.. I wonder when Hollywood will learn the classic writing rule "show don"t tell" ...having plotpoints rammed down your throat with minimal effort of just patching the storyline together with tape, and the fact that nothing will be allowed to stay ambiguous or mysterious. Geez!! Without spoiling too much... don't go  hoping for the crescendic conclusion to this forcefully chosen storyline one would have come to expect, and perhaps, you won't end up too disappointed. This third movie, even more than its predecessor, is plagued with identity problems, and unfortunately all magic have been lost in the process.
textOriginal: This movie was truly sad.. I wonder when Hollywood will learn the classic writing rule "show don"t tell" ...having plotpoints rammed down your throat with minimal effort of just patching the storyline together with tape, and the fact that nothing will be allowed to stay ambiguous or mysterious. Geez!! Without spoiling too much... don't go  hoping for the crescendic conclusion to this forcefully chosen storyline one would have come to expect, and perhaps, you won't end up too disappointed. This third movie, even more than its predecessor, is plagued with identity problems, and unfortunately all magic have been lost in the process.

textDisplay: I was worried that the cancel culture ruined the films by forcing Johnny Depp out of the role, but now that I see this new guy it makes me realize that Johnny Depp was the wrong choice to begin with. Dumbledore and Grindelwald were supposedly best friends, and I just don't see Johnny Depp as that best friend match for dumbledore. He's too cartoonish and there's just something about him that does not look right.  Jude and Mads are a good match and you could imagine them as teenagers together much more easily than Johnny Depp.
textOriginal: I was worried that the cancel culture ruined the films by forcing Johnny Depp out of the role, but now that I see this new guy it makes me realize that Johnny Depp was the wrong choice to begin with. Dumbledore and Grindelwald were supposedly best friends, and I just don't see Johnny Depp as that best friend match for dumbledore. He's too cartoonish and there's just something about him that does not look right.  Jude and Mads are a good match and you could imagine them as teenagers together much more easily than Johnny Depp.

textDisplay: I know people are mad because Johnny Depp has been replaced but it's not fair not to give Mads Mikkelsen a chance. People act like Johnny Depp has been playing Grindelwald for years when he really hasn't. The character of Grindelwald has been played by 3 different actors. Colin Farrell played Grindelwald in the first movie. Yeah Johnny Depp was the real character but he only appeared for 10 seconds with a single line. I think people need to get over what happened with Johnny Depp. Yeah what happened to him was unfortunate but it's not a reason to drag the entire series down because of it. People act like Mads Mikkelsen is a terrible actor when he's not.

textDisplay: I’ve got some lovely spoilers that I would like to share, so those who have seen the movie can look in the replies, or if you just want some spoilers… be careful.
textOriginal: I’ve got some lovely spoilers that I would like to share, so those who have seen the movie can look in the replies, or if you just want some spoilers… be careful.

textDisplay:
1. THE QILIN IS NY NEW FAVOURITE ANIMAL, IT IS SO CUTE AND I LOVE IT TO MY OWN HEART
2. Credence and Aberforth, I did not see that coming, and it makes a bit more sense. Much more logical.
3. Loving Queenie’s fashion in this. I know it’s because she’s in a dark place (literally) but still, nice clothes. 
4. Glad Tina appeared near the end. And I’m happy for her promotion.
5. My bf called it that Ariana was an obscurus. 
6. The sexual tension with Grindelwald and Dumbledore pffffft, and at the end especially
7. Jacob and Queenie got married yay. Love that for them.
8. I love the Niffler’s name! Teddy!!
9. The bowtruckle is still as legendary as ever.
10. The Scorpion scene was the best thing to ever happen.
11. Jacob’s anger issues to be released to the entire world is amazing.
12. Lally is a freaking legend and I love her
That’s all I’ve got so far :D
textOriginal:
1. THE QILIN IS NY NEW FAVOURITE ANIMAL, IT IS SO CUTE AND I LOVE IT TO MY OWN HEART
2. Credence and Aberforth, I did not see that coming, and it makes a bit more sense. Much more logical.
3. Loving Queenie’s fashion in this. I know it’s because she’s in a dark place (literally) but still, nice clothes. 
4. Glad Tina appeared near the end. And I’m happy for her promotion.
5. My bf called it that Ariana was an obscurus. 
6. The sexual tension with Grindelwald and Dumbledore pffffft, and at the end especially
7. Jacob and Queenie got married yay. Love that for them.
8. I love the Niffler’s name! Teddy!!
9. The bowtruckle is still as legendary as ever.
10. The Scorpion scene was the best thing to ever happen.
11. Jacob’s anger issues to be released to the entire world is amazing.
12. Lally is a freaking legend and I love her
That’s all I’ve got so far :D

textDisplay: Is that a bad April Fool's joke? I mean he may be a good actor but ... better than Johnny?! 🤣🤣 that doesn't exist, Grindelwald doesn't even look like Grindelwald anymore and so it's Johnny's way to empathise with the characters and really become one with them, which makes his movies so special. I think no other actor Johnny manages and remains the best not only as an actor but as a person you just have to love him. It's just terrible what can happen to such a loving man and even if he slowly comes back to himself, he is far from finished with this topic, the process will tear up many old wounds again and take away all his strength. But luckily he knows that we all stick to him and he is not alone in the end, whatever happens. For my part, I will always believe in his version of the story no matter what the judges say. Drugs and alcohol or not a person who is good at heart can't change such things so blatantly in terms of character and as understanding and calm as he treats his fans makes it almost impossible to believe this nonsense what AH says there. I have never been able to meet him in person but even in videos he radiates such peace and warmth that you feel directly safe aggressive people can not generate such a charisma whether sober or not if you have a little knowledge of human nature you can read it and you realise that he is really introverted and shy which is not due to drugs, I am extremely introverted myself and also start to stutter completely and can no longer get a straight sentence out if I am supposed to speak in front of strangers. AH has exactly this cold and aggressive charisma, she is only trimmed for success and Werder was interested in Johnny (already in his money) in her fans, she was really jealous when Johnny spent time with fans as if she knew because we give him strength to fight her🙄🙄🙄. Anyway, I'm sorry I wasted your time, that's just my own opinion and I accept any other opinion as well. I will definitely always support Johnny, especially because I know exactly what it feels like to stand alone and not to know what to do next and I never want to experience how the other happens, especially since he has already become depressed by this message and has probably injured himself out of despair (which also speaks for him that he prefers to omit his pain in himself rather than to the Personen which gave it to him )

textDisplay:
John 3:16
[16]For God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son, that whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have everlasting life.

Luke 13:3
[3]I tell you, Nay: but, except ye repent, ye shall all likewise perish.

John 14:6
[6]Jesus saith unto him, I am the way, the truth, and the life: no man cometh unto the Father, but by me.

Matthew 18:11 
For the Son of man is come to save that which was lost.

John 8:12
[12]Then spake Jesus again unto them, saying, I am the light of the world: he that followeth me shall not walk in darkness, but shall have the light of life. 

Matthew 4:17
 From that time Jesus began to preach, and to say, Repent: for the kingdom of heaven is at hand.

Romans 10:9 
That if thou shalt confess with thy mouth the Lord Jesus, and shalt believe in thine heart that God hath raised him from the dead, thou shalt be saved.

Romans 10:13
For whosoever shall call upon the name of the Lord shall be saved. 

Luke 6:27-38
But I say unto you which hear, Love your enemies, do good to them which hate you, 
6:28 Bless them that curse you, and pray for them which despitefully use you. 
6:29 And unto him that smiteth thee on the one cheek offer also the other; and him that taketh away thy cloak forbid not to take thy coat also. 
6:30 Give to every man that asketh of thee; and of him that taketh away thy goods ask them not again. 
6:31 And as ye would that men should do to you, do ye also to them likewise. 
6:32 For if ye love them which love you, what thank have ye? for sinners also love those that love them. 
6:33 And if ye do good to them which do good to you, what thank have ye? for sinners also do even the same. 
6:34 And if ye lend to them of whom ye hope to receive, what thank have ye? for sinners also lend to sinners, to receive as much again. 
6:35 But love ye your enemies, and do good, and lend, hoping for nothing again; and your reward shall be great, and ye shall be the children of the Highest: for he is kind unto the unthankful and to the evil. 
6:36 Be ye therefore merciful, as your Father also is merciful. 
6:37 Judge not, and ye shall not be judged: condemn not, and ye shall not be condemned: forgive, and ye shall be forgiven: 
6:38 Give, and it shall be given unto you; good measure, pressed down, and shaken together, and running over, shall men give into your bosom. For with the same measure that ye mete withal it shall be measured to you again.


Ephesians 2:8-9
For by grace are ye saved through faith; and that not of yourselves: it is the gift of God:
Not of works, lest any man should boast.

2 Chronicles 7:14
If my people, which are called by my name, shall humble themselves, and pray, and seek my face, and turn from their wicked ways; then will I hear from heaven, and will forgive their sin, and will heal their land.

Matthew 5:2-12
King James Version
2 And he opened his mouth, and taught them, saying,
3 Blessed are the poor in spirit: for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
4 Blessed are they that mourn: for they shall be comforted.
5 Blessed are the meek: for they shall inherit the earth.
6 Blessed are they which do hunger and thirst after righteousness: for they shall be filled.
7 Blessed are the merciful: for they shall obtain mercy.
8 Blessed are the pure in heart: for they shall see God.
9 Blessed are the peacemakers: for they shall be called the children of God.
10 Blessed are they which are persecuted for righteousness' sake: for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
11 Blessed are ye, when men shall revile you, and persecute you, and shall say all manner of evil against you falsely, for my sake.
12 Rejoice, and be exceeding glad: for great is your reward in heaven: for so persecuted they the prophets which were before you.

Matthew 24:4-14 
And Jesus answered and said unto them, Take heed that no man deceive you.
24:5 For many shall come in my name, saying, I am Christ; and shall deceive many.
24:6 And ye shall hear of wars and rumours of wars: see that ye be not troubled: for all these things must come to pass, but the end is not yet.
24:7 For nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom: and there shall be famines, and pestilences, and earthquakes, in divers places.
24:8 All these are the beginning of sorrows.
24:9 Then shall they deliver you up to be afflicted, and shall kill you: and ye shall be hated of all nations for my name's sake.
24:10 And then shall many be offended, and shall betray one another, and shall hate one another.
24:11 And many false prophets shall rise, and shall deceive many.
24:12 And because iniquity shall abound, the love of many shall wax cold.
24:13 But he that shall endure unto the end, the same shall be saved.
24:14 And this gospel of the kingdom shall be preached in all the world for a witness unto all nations; and then shall the end come.

textDisplay:
I never cared for johnny Depp as Grindelwald. I just felt that role wasnt right for him..
The charisma was all wrong, his look was ok, and the "twist" at the end of the first fantastic beasts film was seen lightyears away.
I feel Collin farrel wouldve been a better choice than depp...
Now that M.M. is in the role, we may get that friendly horror and charismatic vibe that grindelwald radiated.
I feel he is going to knock this one out of the park. HE is a fantastic actor that deserves more recognition.
I'm still iffy about there being a new Dumbledore in the family... I hope it wont he some tacky writing that explains him..
But I think overall this is going to be epic.
I cant wait to see the extremely hyped up (according to harry potter lore) duel between the two magical juggernauts.
textOriginal:
I never cared for johnny Depp as Grindelwald. I just felt that role wasnt right for him..
The charisma was all wrong, his look was ok, and the "twist" at the end of the first fantastic beasts film was seen lightyears away.
I feel Collin farrel wouldve been a better choice than depp...
Now that M.M. is in the role, we may get that friendly horror and charismatic vibe that grindelwald radiated.
I feel he is going to knock this one out of the park. HE is a fantastic actor that deserves more recognition.
I'm still iffy about there being a new Dumbledore in the family... I hope it wont he some tacky writing that explains him..
But I think overall this is going to be epic.
I cant wait to see the extremely hyped up (according to harry potter lore) duel between the two magical juggernauts.

textDisplay: Kind of wish they had hired Mads from the beginning. I’m already 1000x more invested* in his character now. Don’t get me wrong. I absolutely love Johnny Depp. He’s one of my favorite character actors. But Grindelwald is (to me) a villain steeped more in realism. Brilliant and charismatic. The studios and Johnny’s first approach with the appearance and character seemed too campy to me for the character. More like playing the part instead of being the part? Idk if that makes any sense. I can’t look at the first 2 movies and see Grindelwald, I just see Johnny. I think the studio messed up hiding Johnny for the majority of the first movie. Didn’t give us time to connect with him as the character. They still could have done the big shocking reveal but let us see him Johnny and Colin throughout the movie and then reveal it was the same person the entire time. Would have been more of a red herring as well. Alas! Hope Johnny is doing well! Looking forward to seeing Mads! Hope there’s finally some gayness in this movie!!!! xoxo
textOriginal: Kind of wish they had hired Mads from the beginning. I’m already 1000x more invested* in his character now. Don’t get me wrong. I absolutely love Johnny Depp. He’s one of my favorite character actors. But Grindelwald is (to me) a villain steeped more in realism. Brilliant and charismatic. The studios and Johnny’s first approach with the appearance and character seemed too campy to me for the character. More like playing the part instead of being the part? Idk if that makes any sense. I can’t look at the first 2 movies and see Grindelwald, I just see Johnny. I think the studio messed up hiding Johnny for the majority of the first movie. Didn’t give us time to connect with him as the character. They still could have done the big shocking reveal but let us see him Johnny and Colin throughout the movie and then reveal it was the same person the entire time. Would have been more of a red herring as well. Alas! Hope Johnny is doing well! Looking forward to seeing Mads! Hope there’s finally some gayness in this movie!!!! xoxo

textDisplay:
Mads Mikkelsen is a fantastic actor. Way more versatile & evocative than I’ve ever seen Depp be aside from a few of his early films. Depp may be culturally beloved for his character-acting, but Grindelwald is not supposed to be a “character,” with the panache & odd-emphasis Depp brings to all his roles de rigueur. Grindelwald is a talented, captivating man—he needs to have a quiet burning—the burning of his belief—that draws others to him. 

Mads has repeatedly demonstrated that he can play roles which require this. I would direct anyone who has doubts to search “Hannibal,” the television show, on YT right now. In every scene in Hannibal, Mads draws focus onto his body & into his eyes. His background with dance has allowed him more control & awareness of his movement than other actors. His ability to evoke, even when his face is still, is perfect for Grindelwald. Give him a chance to prove his merit, at least.

Juxtaposition of Depp’s theatrical Grindelwald with Mads’ grounded portrayal so far has sold me on Mads. I liked Depp for the role as well, though I had some criticisms then, having followed press surrounding the film pre-release. Depp didn’t understand—as I think is clear from interviews he’s given—what exactly made Grindelwald so terrifying. Why he’s meant to be at once a savior & a blight—a provocateur & a dictator—a giver & a taker. He’s not childlike, nor is he a dreamer as Depp claims. Depp’s Grindelwald haircut—reminiscent of Hitler youth—isn’t supposed to be the only indication of his sadistic & narcissistic nature. His complete heterochromia isn’t indicative of a dual nature, or an internal war inside him, which Depp again claims—this was clearly an attempt by Depp & the filmmakers to distinguish his appearance & render him into a villainy that’s more recognizable—& marketable.

The cost of Depp’s cartoonish portrayal (& cosmetic) is that as a villain, he becomes indistinguishable from Voldemort. Grindelwald is not Voldemort—they’re separated by a few distinctions, though only one of those is the most important: empathy. Voldemort is unable to feel empathy for others; this leads to his pure-blood supremacy. Grindelwald, in contrast, is shown to feel remorse over crimes committed; it’s suggested that he sees violence as a tool, rather than a pleasurable act. When we lose the emphasis on this distinction—& Grindelwald blends into Voldemort—we lose the entire point of Grindelwald’s character.

Voldemort represented the frightening reality of fanatic & powerful men, who misuse & abuse their power with abandon. His manic desire to cull half-bloods & muggles is what made him so terrifying; the odds of defeating him seemed ever insurmountable. Grindelwald’s terror, however, is less blatant. Unlike Voldemort’s cold & alien visage, Grindelwald has little to distinguish him (by way of appearance) as a “villain.” He looks like anyone else. His abnormalities are solely internal. And that’s the whole point: Grindelwald is meant to appear relatable, approachable. We’re meant to understand how he convinces so many good people to join him. His familiarity is what separates him from Voldemort—a man who neither looked nor acted human. Grindelwald is the reality of powerful men, powerful dictators, & the ease with which they manipulate thousands into believing their rhetoric. No embellishments, no funhouse alterations. His terror comes from how easily he could be a friend, or a neighbor, or co-worker. *His terror lies in how easy it could be to become him.* 

I understand those who haven’t read the books may feel differently, & that’s okay. Keep in mind, in the books, Grindelwald does not disclose the Elden Wand’s location to Voldemort. He defies Voldemort & mocks him; he never gives up Dumbledore, which then leads to Grindelwald’s death. Grindelwald’s film counterpart is more outward in his villainy, which never really fit. His relationship with Dumbledore as young men is part of what has kept him human—whether it’s fear or residual love that he has for Dumbledore, it keeps him from succumbing to arrogance like Voldemort did. Based on all of that, everything from the books, Mads is more suited to this character. In fact, I believe Mads should’ve had this role from the beginning. I know a lot of people will feel the same way once they see the film, if they can go in with an open-mind.

As well, can anyone imagine Jude Law’s Dumbledore having feelings for Johnny Depp’s Grindelwald, & vice versa? I can’t. I also can’t imagine Depp’s Grindelwald being able to charm Law’s Dumbledore into ignorance—where Dumbledore is too in love to realize how dangerous Grindelwald is. I can see it with Mikkelsen much more.

Grindelwald, in contrast, is shown to feel remorse over crimes committed; it’s suggested that he sees violence as a tool, rather than a pleasurable act. When we lose the emphasis on this distinction—& Grindelwald blends into Voldemort—we lose the entire point of Grindelwald’s character.

Voldemort represented the frightening reality of fanatic & powerful men, who misuse & abuse their power with abandon. His manic desire to cull half-bloods & muggles is what made him so terrifying; the odds of defeating him seemed ever insurmountable. Grindelwald’s terror, however, is less blatant. Unlike Voldemort’s cold & alien visage, Grindelwald has little to distinguish him (by way of appearance) as a “villain.” He looks like anyone else. His abnormalities are solely internal. And that’s the whole point: Grindelwald is meant to appear relatable, approachable. We’re meant to understand how he convinces so many good people to join him. His familiarity is what separates him from Voldemort—a man who neither looked nor acted human. Grindelwald is the reality of powerful men, powerful dictators, & the ease with which they manipulate thousands into believing their rhetoric. No embellishments, no funhouse alterations. His terror comes from how easily he could be a friend, or a neighbor, or co-worker. *His terror lies in how easy it could be to become him.* 

I understand those who haven’t read the books may feel differently, & that’s okay. Keep in mind, in the books, Grindelwald does not disclose the Elden Wand’s location to Voldemort. He defies Voldemort & mocks him; he never gives up Dumbledore, which then leads to Grindelwald’s death. Grindelwald’s film counterpart is more outward in his villainy, which never really fit. His relationship with Dumbledore as young men is part of what has kept him human—whether it’s fear or residual love that he has for Dumbledore, it keeps him from succumbing to arrogance like Voldemort did. Based on all of that, everything from the books, Mads is more suited to this character. In fact, I believe Mads should’ve had this role from the beginning. I know a lot of people will feel the same way once they see the film, if they can go in with an open-mind.

As well, can anyone imagine Jude Law’s Dumbledore having feelings for Johnny Depp’s Grindelwald, & vice versa? I can’t. I also can’t imagine Depp’s Grindelwald being able to charm Law’s Dumbledore into ignorance—where Dumbledore is too in love to realize how dangerous Grindelwald is. I can see it with Mikkelsen much more.

textDisplay: Honestly I didn't really think replacing Depp was a big deal when I found out about it, but now seeing this trailer, I really think it's a big loss for the franchise. Mikkelsen is a great actor by his own right, but the Grindlewald played by Depp had his unhinged maniac aura about him, even when he was saying and doing serious things, and the Grindlewald in this trailer just seems...serious. I don't know how to explain it, it's like his cockiness strikes more like the cockiness of a lawyer or politician than a deranged wizard-hitler, it would have been fine if Grindlewald's character was designed like that from the start but it wasn't. I'd be happy to be proven wrong when the movie comes out but I don't think there's any other actor who does "fanatical" like Depp does it, it's in his bones.
textOriginal: Honestly I didn't really think replacing Depp was a big deal when I found out about it, but now seeing this trailer, I really think it's a big loss for the franchise. Mikkelsen is a great actor by his own right, but the Grindlewald played by Depp had his unhinged maniac aura about him, even when he was saying and doing serious things, and the Grindlewald in this trailer just seems...serious. I don't know how to explain it, it's like his cockiness strikes more like the cockiness of a lawyer or politician than a deranged wizard-hitler, it would have been fine if Grindlewald's character was designed like that from the start but it wasn't. I'd be happy to be proven wrong when the movie comes out but I don't think there's any other actor who does "fanatical" like Depp does it, it's in his bones.

textDisplay:
Honestly. I'm half intrigued half put out. Mads just looks bland and boring as Grindelwald. If he at least resembled Johnny depp's Grindelwald the slightest bit, it would be easier to swallow. So He could be played by Amber Hard for all I care. that's what i see when Grindelwald is on scene. It just is too stark a contrast to the previous movies. add in completely different mannerisms. Npo thank you. Honestly how anyone could watch the movie with a clear conscience is beyond me. 
I don't care of Mads is the better Grindelwald ( I don't think so, I loved Johnny's take but just for the sake of the argument) Johnny didn't get replaced for a better actor (something I usually already dislike) but because of false accusations. I do not think I'll be able to watch the movie thinking about anything. If they want to tell the story, they Imho should write books about the fantastic beast series. I'd be able to at least enjoy those. The last three movies will probably close to absolutely unenjoyable. No matter how good the movie looks how intriguing the plothooks sound. The movie just feels wrong on an absolutely elemental level to me.
textOriginal:
Honestly. I'm half intrigued half put out. Mads just looks bland and boring as Grindelwald. If he at least resembled Johnny depp's Grindelwald the slightest bit, it would be easier to swallow. So He could be played by Amber Hard for all I care. that's what i see when Grindelwald is on scene. It just is too stark a contrast to the previous movies. add in completely different mannerisms. Npo thank you. Honestly how anyone could watch the movie with a clear conscience is beyond me. 
I don't care of Mads is the better Grindelwald ( I don't think so, I loved Johnny's take but just for the sake of the argument) Johnny didn't get replaced for a better actor (something I usually already dislike) but because of false accusations. I do not think I'll be able to watch the movie thinking about anything. If they want to tell the story, they Imho should write books about the fantastic beast series. I'd be able to at least enjoy those. The last three movies will probably close to absolutely unenjoyable. No matter how good the movie looks how intriguing the plothooks sound. The movie just feels wrong on an absolutely elemental level to me.

textDisplay:
I am a huge Harry Potter fan but literally nothing about this trailer excites me. The last two movies also deeply disappointed me. Mostly because they are made by JK Rowling, herself, and she is polluting the magic of her own world with confusing and underwhelming movies with plots and facts contrary to the original story. She is pulling a Season 8 of “Game of Thrones”. Taking something amazing and dropping a lit match on it. Like many, I grew up with these characters and these movies so it makes me very sad to see these new movies be so unapologetically inaccurate and blasé. It’s dramatic but it feels like she cast Sectumsempra on the franchise, as a whole, and it’s slowly but surely bleeding out before our very eyes. 

Because I’m a loyal Hufflepuff, I will still probably go and see it but if Jacob, a muggle, gets a wand, I will leave the theater and never watch a new Harry Potter movie again. The two most fundamental laws of magic are that only which is in wizards can use wands and that the wand chooses the wizard. But a muggle baker gets a wand as a present and I am expected to just believe that it works fine for him? Voldemort was insanely powerful and even he couldn’t fully use the Elder Wand but Jacob can just grab one and go?? If that’s what happens, I am officially done with the new movies and I’ll stick to the classics forevermore. 

But thank god they got rid of Johnny Depp! I hated his rendition of the character in every way! After “Pirates of the Caribbean”, he plays every character exactly the same way and I am so sick of it so that’s the only silver lining I see so far! 🤷🏻‍♂️
textOriginal:
I am a huge Harry Potter fan but literally nothing about this trailer excites me. The last two movies also deeply disappointed me. Mostly because they are made by JK Rowling, herself, and she is polluting the magic of her own world with confusing and underwhelming movies with plots and facts contrary to the original story. She is pulling a Season 8 of “Game of Thrones”. Taking something amazing and dropping a lit match on it. Like many, I grew up with these characters and these movies so it makes me very sad to see these new movies be so unapologetically inaccurate and blasé. It’s dramatic but it feels like she cast Sectumsempra on the franchise, as a whole, and it’s slowly but surely bleeding out before our very eyes. 

Because I’m a loyal Hufflepuff, I will still probably go and see it but if Jacob, a muggle, gets a wand, I will leave the theater and never watch a new Harry Potter movie again. The two most fundamental laws of magic are that only which is in wizards can use wands and that the wand chooses the wizard. But a muggle baker gets a wand as a present and I am expected to just believe that it works fine for him? Voldemort was insanely powerful and even he couldn’t fully use the Elder Wand but Jacob can just grab one and go?? If that’s what happens, I am officially done with the new movies and I’ll stick to the classics forevermore. 

But thank god they got rid of Johnny Depp! I hated his rendition of the character in every way! After “Pirates of the Caribbean”, he plays every character exactly the same way and I am so sick of it so that’s the only silver lining I see so far! 🤷🏻‍♂️

textDisplay:
Arguably Tina was the most important character in the first film.  She had much less screentime in number 2, and it was all over the place. 
Newt is awkward, but he connects with Tina. She's the practical side of the partnership. 
Queenie cannot be redeemed without Tina. Jacob isn't enough to ground her. 
She had an established connection with Credence, and her trust in Newt helped him to trust in Newt.
Even Grindelwald, as Graves, had a connection with her. 

Sidelining Tina is a bad plan.
textOriginal:
Arguably Tina was the most important character in the first film.  She had much less screentime in number 2, and it was all over the place. 
Newt is awkward, but he connects with Tina. She's the practical side of the partnership. 
Queenie cannot be redeemed without Tina. Jacob isn't enough to ground her. 
She had an established connection with Credence, and her trust in Newt helped him to trust in Newt.
Even Grindelwald, as Graves, had a connection with her. 

Sidelining Tina is a bad plan.

textDisplay: I might be making a premature assumption here, since the movie isn't out yet. But isn't Grindelwald's war is against the magical world, and not the muggles? As far as understand, the difference between Voldemort and Grindelwald is that Voldemort wants to dominate the world opressing muggles to become servants of witches and wizards because he sees them as inferior race. But Grindlewald seeks to liberate the magical world from their statute of secrecy so that he can rule the magical world alongside muggles, so they can live together in harmony. So he wages war against pro-secrecy wizards with his pro-liberation followers.

textDisplay: aside from them re-casting the actor to play Grindlewald, my only complaint regarding the trailer is that Dumbledore GAVE Kewalski his wand when it has been made extremely clear that the wand chooses the wizard or witch.  So I guess they are just throwing the rule-book out the window at this point and doing whatever with this series.  The only way I can see the wand working is if it was a fake ward that has been charmed to work with certain hand gestures by the user who is in fact a muggle.  Maybe Dumbledore gifted this "fake" wand to Kewalski to help convince other witches and wizards that he IS a wizard and therefore shouldn't be mind-wiped as muggles so often must be during this mission.
textOriginal: aside from them re-casting the actor to play Grindlewald, my only complaint regarding the trailer is that Dumbledore GAVE Kewalski his wand when it has been made extremely clear that the wand chooses the wizard or witch. The only way I can see the wand working is if it was a fake ward that has been charmed to work with certain hand gestures by the user who is in fact a muggle. textDisplay:
I'm sorry but no. This has no business to be a "Fantastic Beasts" movie. 
With Dumbledore and Grindelwald clashing, this could've been a perfect wizarding spy/war movie, something along the lines of "Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Spy" but the overall vibe is just ruined by Newt and his world. His presence muddles everything, and steals focus from the two main characters way too much. 

Shame.
textOriginal:
I'm sorry but no. This has no business to be a "Fantastic Beasts" movie. 
With Dumbledore and Grindelwald clashing, this could've been a perfect wizarding spy/war movie, something along the lines of "Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Spy" but the overall vibe is just ruined by Newt and his world. His presence muddles everything, and steals focus from the two main characters way too much. 

Shame.

textDisplay: I can't wait to see the movie, but the recasting of Depp was stupid and kills some of my enthusiasm for the movie, as it would for any other movie recasting a lead. It wouldn't be so bad if they'd at least made an effort to create a similar look for the new cast, but they went from a guy who looks like a villain to Walter White. Just a terrible choice to make once a precedent had been set.
textOriginal: I can't wait to see the movie, but the recasting of Depp was stupid and kills some of my enthusiasm for the movie, as it would for any other movie recasting a lead. It wouldn't be so bad if they'd at least made an effort to create a similar look for the new cast, but they went from a guy who looks like a villain to Walter White. Just a terrible choice to make once a precedent had been set.

...um is it just me or did the makeup team basically Hitler-ized Mads (minus the goatee)???

also I guess the theories of Jacob being a Squib must be gaining ground by now, as it's being obvious that he can see Hogwarts as it is and not just a smouldering ruin with a Keep Out sign

plus is Grindelwald recruiting the Nationalists (Kuomintang) or the Communists (who need I remind are not only atheists but also launched the Cultural Revolution to "Destroy the Four Old's")?

so the banner reads "堅決支持劉洮選為 國際魔法協會會長", and I have no idea on the first half, but the 2nd half is basically "International Confederation of Wizards" (despite the official "Harry Potter" translation as "國際巫師聯合會")
and apparently in the 1st trailer the 2nd half originally read "國際魔術協會主席"

textDisplay: In the Room of Requirement you can see Dumbledore taking Kowalski to what seems like an ancient egyptian bell (?) which is probably a portkey of some sorts to Egypt. Since Egyptians were the first ones to study and use magic, it’s possible that somewhere in Egypt there’s information on how to trigger magic. Since Ancient Egyptians must’ve all been muggles (obviously bc they were the first ones to do magic), there must be a way to unlock Kowalski’s magical potential. It might take a bit longer, but you can see him using magic. Also it’s the room of requirement. There’s 100% going to be a way for Kowalski to become a wizard. That’s my theory at least.

[3]I tell you, Nay: but, except ye repent, ye shall all likewise perish.

Romans 10:13
For whosoever shall call upon the name of the Lord shall be saved. 

Luke 6:27-38
But I say unto you which hear, Love your enemies, do good to them which hate you, 
6:28 Bless them that curse you, and pray for them which despitefully use you. 
6:29 And unto him that smiteth thee on the one cheek offer also the other; and him that taketh away thy cloak forbid not to take thy coat also. 
6:30 Give to every man that asketh of thee; and of him that taketh away thy goods ask them not again. 
6:31 And as ye would that men should do to you, do ye also to them likewise. 
6:32 For if ye love them which love you, what thank have ye? for sinners also love those that love them. 
6:33 And if ye do good to them which do good to you, what thank have ye? for sinners also do even the same. 
6:34 And if ye lend to them of whom ye hope to receive, what thank have ye? for sinners also lend to sinners, to receive as much again. 
6:35 But love ye your enemies, and do good, and lend, hoping for nothing again; and your reward shall be great, and ye shall be the children of the Highest: for he is kind unto the unthankful and to the evil. 
6:36 Be ye therefore merciful, as your Father also is merciful. 
6:37 Judge not, and ye shall not be judged: condemn not, and ye shall not be condemned: forgive, and ye shall be forgiven: 
6:38 Give, and it shall be given unto you; good measure, pressed down, and shaken together, and running over, shall men give into your bosom. For with the same measure that ye mete withal it shall be measured to you again.


Ephesians 2:8-9

Not of works, lest any man should boast.

2 Chronicles 7:14
If my people, which are called by my name, shall humble themselves, and pray, and seek my face, and turn from their wicked ways; then will I hear from heaven, and will forgive their sin, and will heal their land.

King James Version
2 And he opened his mouth, and taught them, saying,

4 Blessed are they that mourn: for they shall be comforted.
5 Blessed are the meek: for they shall inherit the earth.
6 Blessed are they which do hunger and thirst after righteousness: for they shall be filled.
7 Blessed are the merciful: for they shall obtain mercy.
8 Blessed are the pure in heart: for they shall see God.
9 Blessed are the peacemakers: for they shall be called the children of God.
10 Blessed are they which are persecuted for righteousness' sake: for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
11 Blessed are ye, when men shall revile you, and persecute you, and shall say all manner of evil against you falsely, for my sake.
12 Rejoice, and be exceeding glad: for great is your reward in heaven: for so persecuted they the prophets which were before you.

Matthew 24:4-14 
And Jesus answered and said unto them, Take heed that no man deceive you.
24:5 For many shall come in my name, saying, I am Christ; and shall deceive many.
24:6 And ye shall hear of wars and rumours of wars: see that ye be not troubled: for all these things must come to pass, but the end is not yet.
24:7 For nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom: and there shall be famines, and pestilences, and earthquakes, in divers places.
24:8 All these are the beginning of sorrows.
24:9 Then shall they deliver you up to be afflicted, and shall kill you: and ye shall be hated of all nations for my name's sake.
24:10 And then shall many be offended, and shall betray one another, and shall hate one another.
24:11 And many false prophets shall rise, and shall deceive many.
24:12 And because iniquity shall abound, the love of many shall wax cold.
24:13 But he that shall endure unto the end, the same shall be saved.
24:14 And this gospel of the kingdom shall be preached in all the world for a witness unto all nations; and then shall the end come.

textDisplay: I honestly never expected to like these films. But I was pleasantly surprised when I finally forced myself to watch them. Still not the biggest Johnny depp fan though. But the character is really good. I still wish they’d make a prequel following Voldemort, from HIS perspective, to learn more of his story, with a much, much, darker and more mature tone. They always talked about how dark the times were, but we really didn’t see that… even the finale of HP was pretty tame imo.
textOriginal: I honestly never expected to like these films. But I was pleasantly surprised when I finally forced myself to watch them. Still not the biggest Johnny depp fan though. But the character is really good. I still wish they’d make a prequel following Voldemort, from HIS perspective, to learn more of his story, with a much, much, darker and more mature tone. They always talked about how dark the times were, but we really didn’t see that… even the finale of HP was pretty tame imo.

textDisplay: To me, Fantastic Beasts somehow feels like a modernized Wizarding World, while in Harry Potter, everything (mostly) feels more medieval-fantasy, despite taking place later. For example, the clothes. Everyone here is dressing up modern, wearing suits or dresses. In Harry Potter, they all wear the old-fashioned robes and hats. Best example there is Dumbledore. I really cant see him having worn a suit in the past and then later decide to take a robe when he gets older. Feels like the whole wizarding world completely devolved somehow.
textOriginal: To me, Fantastic Beasts somehow feels like a modernized Wizarding World, while in Harry Potter, everything (mostly) feels more medieval-fantasy, despite taking place later. For example, the clothes. Everyone here is dressing up modern, wearing suits or dresses. In Harry Potter, they all wear the old-fashioned robes and hats. Best example there is Dumbledore. I really cant see him having worn a suit in the past and then later decide to take a robe when he gets older. Feels like the whole wizarding world completely devolved somehow.

textDisplay: Young Dumbledore probably had a lot more time to dress in muggle clothes as a teacher. The older you get the more practical you'd want to be and robes are just so convenient. I'm a fashion and costume designer and it makes sense to me since Dumbledore and the teachers basically lived 24/7 in Hogwarts.  Also look at old men with big minds like Einstein, he stopped putting so much effort into dressing up differently everyday and he just bought many of the same items and wore a sort of uniform. And for the kids well, Hogwart's uniforms haven't changed in centuries so, I think robes make sense. These adult wizards have to blend in the muggle society. Their suits aren't "modern". Suits are classic and have become a standard for many decades. In the 1930's they were very popular amongst women and men, since both started working on bigger corporations and in big cities. If you look at the Harry Potter movies where they go to the Ministry of Magic you see most wizards are wearing suits. It's still the standard for wizards and witches office workers.
textOriginal: Young Dumbledore probably had a lot more time to dress in muggle clothes as a teacher. The older you get the more practical you'd want to be and robes are just so convenient. Also look at old men with big minds like Einstein, he stopped putting so much effort into dressing up differently everyday and he just bought many of the same items and wore a sort of uniform. And for the kids well, Hogwart's uniforms haven't changed in centuries so, I think robes make sense. These adult wizards have to blend in the muggle society. Their suits aren't "modern". Suits are classic and have become a standard for many decades. In the 1930's they were very popular amongst women and men, since both started working on bigger corporations and in big cities. textDisplay: the best way i can explain it is there is a book within the harry potter universe called 'fantastic beasts and where to find them and it was written but a person called newt scammander. we see how newt learned about magical creatures and writes a book about them (we see this in the first film). and we also see how dumbledore defeats grindelwald which was mentioned many times throughout the harry potter series. so in summary there are three fantastic beasts films that are set before the harry potter books (the order of the films are: fantastic beasts and where to find them, fantasic beasts the crimes of grindelwald, fantastic beast the secrets of dumbledore) and yes we do see a few characters and known names pop up in these films but i dont want to spoil it too much
textOriginal: the best way i can explain it is there is a book within the harry potter universe called 'fantastic beasts and where to find them and it was written but a person called newt scammander. we see how newt learned about magical creatures and writes a book about them (we see this in the first film). and we also see how dumbledore defeats grindelwald which was mentioned many times throughout the harry potter series. so in summary there are three fantastic beasts films that are set before the harry potter books (the order of the films are: fantastic beasts and where to find them, fantasic beasts the crimes of grindelwald, fantastic beast the secrets of dumbledore) and yes we do see a few characters and known names pop up in these films but i dont want to spoil it too much

textDisplay: Mhh i love Mads and i love the harry potter universe, but this will likely disappoint me. The Trilogy is missing charm and plot. To many substories, to many "jokes" to get 6 year olds to laugh, while still trying to be serious (Grindelwald is a very serious topic). I think you either make it a kids movie and let the new generation grow up with it, or you make it a movie aimed at the original harry potter audience and continue from there. I really dislike the effort to satisfy everyone. By trying to be liked by everyone, i think this Trilogy will end up being liked by very few people. Praise to the entire team for working so hard to give us what we want, but i think the writers are missing the goal.
textOriginal: Mhh i love Mads and i love the harry potter universe, but this will likely disappoint me. The Trilogy is missing charm and plot. To many substories, to many "jokes" to get 6 year olds to laugh, while still trying to be serious (Grindelwald is a very serious topic). I think you either make it a kids movie and let the new generation grow up with it, or you make it a movie aimed at the original harry potter audience and continue from there. I really dislike the effort to satisfy everyone. By trying to be liked by everyone, i think this Trilogy will end up being liked by very few people. Praise to the entire team for working so hard to give us what we want, but i think the writers are missing the goal.

textDisplay: For me, the last film was very forgettable (I think there was wayyy too much going on). The only thing I hope for is eventually we get the big Grindlewald vs Dumbledore duel. After all, the storyline was that Dumbledore defeated Grindlewald. I really hope they don't change that and have this group somehow beat Grindlewald. That would make the show very similar to one of my biggest (and only) complains about Harry Potter: I wish they had made Harry stronger. It would have been cool to see a big duel but it was a disappointing for me to see the usual avada kedavra vs expelliarmus duel. I really hope they don't do a similar thing in this movie and have a group of wizards and witches who should not be able to beat Grindlewald beat him.
textOriginal: For me, the last film was very forgettable (I think there was wayyy too much going on). The only thing I hope for is eventually we get the big Grindlewald vs Dumbledore duel. After all, the storyline was that Dumbledore defeated Grindlewald. I really hope they don't change that and have this group somehow beat Grindlewald. That would make the show very similar to one of my biggest (and only) complains about Harry Potter: I wish they had made Harry stronger. textDisplay:
Я хочу обратиться к русскому народу. По истории мира, вы были самым грозным государством.
Которая сражалась за свои права, свою нацию, свою землю и свою свободу. Сейчас весь мир смотрит на вас с жалостью и ненавистью. Вы сдали свою позицию грозному народу!
Позицию грозного народа забрала себе Украина, где люди за 10 лет показали, что они за свою свободу, свои права, свое будущее и будущее своих детей готовы отдать свою жизнь. А вы в течении 20 лет сидите в своих домах и жалуетесь на то, что вы не можете влиять на событие своей державы, что вы не выбирали нынешнюю власть!
Да вы позор ваших дедов и прадедов!
В 2012 Украина расплатилась за свой выбор 100-ю жизней, чтобы ее права и ее свобода была у ее граждан.
А за ваши действия сейчас, Русский народ будет расплачиваться больше 100 лет, ваши дети, внуки правнуки будут жить в мире, в котором их ненавидят и презиряють.
Они будут жить на клочке земли по которому вы зараз ходите, и больше нигде, потому что им нигде не будут рады! 
Ваши дети будут всегда бедны, потому что они будут расплачиваться за ваш выбор и за ваши действия. Хотя да ваш народ, сейчас тоже расплачивается за жизнь. Жизнь ваших сыну, которых отправляют на войну в Украину, где они убивают женщин, детей ничему не должны граждан, а потом сами отправляются за ними на тот свет! 
Если вас это устраивает сидите и дальше дома, жалуйтесь на свою безпомочность

А за ваши действия сейчас, Русский народ будет расплачиваться больше 100 лет, ваши дети, внуки правнуки будут жить в мире, в котором их ненавидят и презиряють.
Они будут жить на клочке земли по которому вы зараз ходите, и больше нигде, потому что им нигде не будут рады! 
Ваши дети будут всегда бедны, потому что они будут расплачиваться за ваш выбор и за ваши действия. Хотя да ваш народ, сейчас тоже расплачивается за жизнь. Жизнь ваших сыну, которых отправляют на войну в Украину, где они убивают женщин, детей ничему не должны граждан, а потом сами отправляются за ними на тот свет! 
Если вас это устраивает сидите и дальше дома, жалуйтесь на свою безпомочность

textDisplay: hello wish i live in russia and i want to say from all batman fans in russia we want to watch batman movie. We have been waiting for this film for two years, we created a discord club, we have been discussing the film for a whole year, we have been looking forward to it, and suddenly, on a sunny morning, I read the news that Disney and Warner Bros have canceled the screening of the obsessive films "Turning Red" and "Batman" due to the war between Russia and Ukraine. We've been wiped out we've already bought tickets I'm not angry I'm just upset we've been waiting for the movie for years and we're asking for at least a special screening for a week. Yes, I know this is a war, yes, this is terrible. One of our friends lives in Ukraine and we are afraid for him. He is also very upset that he canceled Batman in Russia. We want to watch the movie "Batman" in Russia!

textDisplay: Yessss I'm stoked to see Mads Mikkelsen as Grindelwald. I was so bummed when they revealed that Johnny Depp was Grindelwald because his character was bizarre, cartoonish and unappealing with his unfortunate issue of acting everything like it's Captain Jack Sparrow these days. Hope he gets the help he needs, but very glad he was removed from the film. Grindelwald is supposed to be monstrous, but also appealing and seductive and Mads is perfect for that! Can't wait to see his chemistry opposite Jude! Hopefully this movie is better than the last and it will be a bit of a re-boot for the franchise. Fingers crossed! Also: Hoping there's as many scenes with Queenie as possible. Alison Sudol is radiant on screen, and I would watch a whole movie with just her character.
textOriginal: Yessss I'm stoked to see Mads Mikkelsen as Grindelwald. I was so bummed when they revealed that Johnny Depp was Grindelwald because his character was bizarre, cartoonish and unappealing with his unfortunate issue of acting everything like it's Captain Jack Sparrow these days. Hope he gets the help he needs, but very glad he was removed from the film. Grindelwald is supposed to be monstrous, but also appealing and seductive and Mads is perfect for that! Can't wait to see his chemistry opposite Jude! Hopefully this movie is better than the last and it will be a bit of a re-boot for the franchise. Fingers crossed! textDisplay: Grindelwald was a total moron. He could have used the imperius curse on the muggle military to make them bomb their own countries in an effort to exterminate them. He could have used ritual magic in every hospital on Earth to make every muggle born in the hospital born infertile, creating a Children of Men post-apocalyptic scenario where muggles can no longer reproduce. He could have come up with a dark magic virus that infected muggles and killed them all. But no…he spends years battling ministries, killing his own kind, losing his own supporters in vicious battles, and further shrinking the already small wizarding population. Absolute moron. One of the worst Dark Lords in history by sheer stupidity.
textOriginal: Grindelwald was a total moron. He could have used the imperius curse on the muggle military to make them bomb their own countries in an effort to exterminate them. He could have used ritual magic in every hospital on Earth to make every muggle born in the hospital born infertile, creating a Children of Men post-apocalyptic scenario where muggles can no longer reproduce. He could have come up with a dark magic virus that infected muggles and killed them all. But no…he spends years battling ministries, killing his own kind, losing his own supporters in vicious battles, and further shrinking the already small wizarding population. Absolute moron. textDisplay:
While I am looking forward to this movie, I'm also extremely nervous about it. Here are some of my concerns:

1. Why does Credence look like Kylo Ren?

2. Why are we back at Hogwarts? I thought this series was all about getting away from Hogwarts and exploring other magical cultures.

3. Is Newt still the main character? We didn't see him until about 36 seconds into the trailer. The fact that we focus so much on Dumbledore makes Newt feel like a side character.

4. Where's Tina?

5. How is this whole series going to work? Fantastic Beasts 2 takes place in September 1927. We know Dumbledore defeats Grindelwald in 1945. That's eighteen years away. We're going to need a massive time skip, but if that's the case, why is Queenie on Grindelwald's side for so long? She can read minds. Surely, she can tell that he's the bad guy. Alternatively, Grindelwald might not even be defeated before the end of the series. J.K. Rowling could be setting this up for a completely different series, where we follow a different main character, like Florean Fortescue, who helps Dumbledore defeat Grindelwald instead.

I don't know. I could be reading way too much into this. I still want to see the movie, but after Fantastic Beasts 2, J.K. Rowling has to earn my trust back.
textOriginal:
While I am looking forward to this movie, I'm also extremely nervous about it. Here are some of my concerns:

1. Why does Credence look like Kylo Ren?

2. Why are we back at Hogwarts? I thought this series was all about getting away from Hogwarts and exploring other magical cultures.

3. Is Newt still the main character? We didn't see him until about 36 seconds into the trailer. The fact that we focus so much on Dumbledore makes Newt feel like a side character.

4. Where's Tina?

5. How is this whole series going to work? Fantastic Beasts 2 takes place in September 1927. We know Dumbledore defeats Grindelwald in 1945. That's eighteen years away. We're going to need a massive time skip, but if that's the case, why is Queenie on Grindelwald's side for so long? She can read minds. Surely, she can tell that he's the bad guy. Alternatively, Grindelwald might not even be defeated before the end of the series. J.K. Rowling could be setting this up for a completely different series, where we follow a different main character, like Florean Fortescue, who helps Dumbledore defeat Grindelwald instead.

I don't know. I could be reading way too much into this. I still want to see the movie, but after Fantastic Beasts 2, J.K. Rowling has to earn my trust back.

textDisplay: I don't think Newt has any business being in this story. He should have his own magizoology TV show, and Jude Law's Dumbledore should lead a film like this. This whole thing is a contrived, convoluted mess.
textOriginal: I don't think Newt has any business being in this story. He should have his own magizoology TV show, and Jude Law's Dumbledore should lead a film like this. This whole thing is a contrived, convoluted mess.

textDisplay:
1. He got yassified XD

2. Well judging by the deeply ignorant additions JKR made while describing other cultures on Pottermore (such as having an Inca temple in Brazil) I think she's better off sticking with england. I have come to realize that the world building in HP isn't actually that great, it's a bunch of interesting ideas thrown together but not only is it very geographically limited, those ideas tend to fall apart when you think too much about it. JKR is good at imagining individual lives, but not so much at understanding and describing complex social systems which is why HP has a lot of named characters and we learn about their individual actions, but the worldbuilding is lacking in big sociological impact and storytelling. She's no Frank Hebert, or Tolkien and it shows but I'm not quite sure she realizes that she would benefit from working with people who would complement her on that front. Especially people who know about the cultures, peoples and languages of the world so she doesn't have to rely on cliches and bad google translate for worldbuilding. (I'm still not over the fact that the wizard school in japan is basically named "magical place". That's beyond lazy for someone who supposedly loves playing with words.). 

3. The fact that the "fantastic beasts" part of the logo is in the smallest font imaginable, compared to the gigantic Dumbledore, kinda furthers the impression I already had after FB2 that this series has nothing to do with them. they are cute pokemons invoked here in there to do stuff that could probably be solved with a spell or some magical artefact but ultimately they serve no thematic purpose after the first movie. It's like... imagine if Harry Potter ended up being about Neville Longbottom after the first book. What is the point? Just name the series after Grindelwald from the start since he is the only element that has relevance in all 3 movies. 

4. Maybe, JKR has finally realized that you can't put as many characters in a 600 pages book and  a 2h movie after FB2 and decided to cut characters that are ultimately not that important to the plot? But I'm not so sure the right lessons were learned after that last movie. Maybe she was omitted from the trailer bc there are in fact too many characters in the story and the editor forgot about her. 

5. Honestly at this point, I wouldn't be surprised if she actuually forgot that the dates don't add up. But maybe as you said the series will really only end after 18 more years have passed. However I doubt there is really that much of a justification for such a big skip in the text itself other than the arbitrary dates she chose. Like I wouldn't be surprised if at the end of the day, what she ends up telling after the 18 years skip could've happened at anytime. This for me, really stems from making a series that is actually about Dumby and his lover, start with pokemon master who really has nothing to do with their specific drama. This series tries to be two things at once when, given how much revenue anything with the HP font can generate, Warner bros would have no problem with throwing money at two different HP related projects. A much better idea for me would've been a series on Dumbledore (since we teased in HP7 how interesting his life was, might as well tell it), and a movie series about fun energetic adventures with Newt and his pokemons traveling the world and making new friends. However that would require letting people other than JKR write as she can't be expected to do both at the same time, and at this point I believe there is simply not as many HP related TV and Movie products as you'd expect from such a succesfull franchise, because she doesn't want to delegate (even when she should). It's a shame really because I do not trust her anymore either after FB2. She's a novelist, not a screenwriter and the lack of cohesion in FB2 is evidence of that.

textDisplay: Fawkes makes his debut! Though im really hoping that it is Fawkes at 2:19 and not Credence phoenix. When I first saw Credence phoenix I thought that it was Fawkes and that Credence might have been defeated or maybe killed so the phoenix went to the next Dumbledore lineage but it didnt sit well with me that THE albus dumbledore would just be a second choice for a phoenix to serve. In the books, Fawkes had no previous owner prior to meeting Dumbledore so I want to believe that they would feature two phoenixes in the films: Credence phoenix and Fawkes. A battle between two phoenixes would be a sight to see.

textDisplay: You know it's really ironic that Dumbledore had to have two different actor's over the course of the movies with Richard Harris widely regarded as the better one. Now here we are 15 plus years later and the big bad of Fantastic Beasts, a character who is intricately connected to Albus Dumbledore, also has to have two different actors. It would be EVEN MORE IRONIC if Mads Miklsen the second actor became the better received performance in a complete reversal of Dumbledore.
textOriginal: You know it's really ironic that Dumbledore had to have two different actor's over the course of the movies with Richard Harris widely regarded as the better one. Now here we are 15 plus years later and the big bad of Fantastic Beasts, a character who is intricately connected to Albus Dumbledore, also has to have two different actors. It would be EVEN MORE IRONIC if Mads Miklsen the second actor became the better received performance in a complete reversal of Dumbledore.

textDisplay: To begin...trailers.. they should stop with all the trailers that are being released for every film. This ruins the experience of the film when you watch it for the first time. 75% of the scenes in the film you've already seen if you watch all the trailers. I stopped long ago watching every trailer, but still people share pictures, I'd say make 1 really good trailer a few months before the release of the film and that's it.  Now secondly, there has been too many unnecessary fandom wars within the Harry Potter fandom, zero respect for each others different opinion. This is a shame because every Harry Potter wants the same thing ,  being hyped about Harry Potter. Having said that I am glad to see so many Harry Potter fans who are enjoying the Fantastic Beasts films and I hope the FB fans will have as much fun with this one as they had with previous films. However for me, I liked the first film I liked the creativity of the first film and the introduction of a few great new characters in the Wizardworld , like Queenie. The second one is were to me it went wrong, the script was such a mess and the changes of some of the canon bothered me. Also wizards wearing muggle clothes in the wizarding World is not a good development. It makes zero sense.  This is ofcourse not new but it is getting worse the longer David Yates stays on board as a director. In the first FB I could accept it because they were in muggle locations. So in short I don't have that Harry Potter feeling which I had with the Original films and that is very unfortunate. Plus the changing to the canon are not good things to me. That is ofcourse my respectful opinion. I will watch the films , but not enjoy them. The great thing with books is that at least you have your own imagination of the characters. As a Harry Potter fan a little more than 20 years now, I wished I was as excited as I used to be with the original films and the books , but it is what it is. I still wish the FB fans lots of fun with the film, even though we may disagree or have a difference of opinion.

textDisplay:
Dear WB.
I speak on behalf of Russian Batman fans, I was waiting for the release of the film as my birthday, we were all waiting for it. In connection with the latest news, with the situation in Ukraine, I want to tell you that you did not insult us, you made us angry. We are not to blame for the decisions of our politicians. All we want is the release of the movie, otherwise we'll watch it anyway, pirate it. You make a mistake and spend extra money! We are fans and we don't want politics to affect our movie and comic universes! Please consider my request. Good luck with the rental!
textOriginal:
Dear WB.
I speak on behalf of Russian Batman fans, I was waiting for the release of the film as my birthday, we were all waiting for it. In connection with the latest news, with the situation in Ukraine, I want to tell you that you did not insult us, you made us angry. We are not to blame for the decisions of our politicians. All we want is the release of the movie, otherwise we'll watch it anyway, pirate it. You make a mistake and spend extra money! We are fans and we don't want politics to affect our movie and comic universes! Please consider my request. Good luck with the rental!

textDisplay:
Another legendary series of films is being created before our eyes. Just as now people born in 2000-2010 envy people born in 1980-1990 that they could experience how the Harry Potter series was created, so people born in 2015-2020 will envy us that we could wait impatiently for every new Fantastic Beasts movie.
Let's enjoy the moment, brothers and sisters, and we will appreciate what we have :)

Just as now people born in 2000-2010 envy people born in 1980-1990 that they could experience how the Harry Potter series was created, so people born in 2015-2020 will envy us that we could wait impatiently for every new Fantastic Beasts movie.
Let's enjoy the moment, brothers and sisters, and we will appreciate what we have :)

textDisplay:
Hello!
I am writing to you from Russia, if not strange. I have read and heard the news that you have canceled the premiere of Batman in Russia. I understand why you canceled. But I want you to understand: we may not get along. I mean the relationship between Russians and Americans. Yes, our president sent troops to Ukraine. BUT! We are not Germans, as in 1941, without reason or warning, we invaded with the war. Our military did not go to fight and drove off. Believe it or not, fascists have appeared in Ukraine. You probably know who the fascists are. Our president wants to warn against this. Our country in 1941 was just preparing for war and tried to warn her, but the Germans suddenly attacked. The President no longer wants to make such a mistake and he took action. I'm not saying that the president is well done and that he is good. All people are essentially good, you just need to understand what exactly is happening.
I will not explain the whole situation to you, I think you will understand. But canceling the release of Batman in Russia is a shot in the heart. We, Russians, as well as Americans, Chinese, Europeans and so on - we all have been waiting for the release of Batman for more than a year. If you attacked Ukraine, would you cancel due to invading a foreign country? Our military invaded not just like that, I already wrote to you from above. We are all living in difficult times right now. God forbid it comes to war. If you read this message, I will be glad. I ask you to reconsider your decision to cancel. If you think that the president acted badly, then what does it have to do with peaceful people? I see no reason to cancel the film in a country where there are peaceful people. Once again I ask you to reconsider your decision to cancel the premiere of the release in our country. Hope you read my post...

I'm 15 years old, I probably wrote badly, I'm just learning English. I wish you all the best and the best :)

textDisplay: I was worried about Mads Mikkelsen replacing Johnny Depp as Grindelwald, but damn, I actually find Mads more terrifying as Grindelwald. He gives off a far more intimidating calm by frightening demeanor than Depp. He looks and feels like even though he's calm as cold steel, he could wreak unspeakable havoc in the blink of an eye and that is something that I think a true villain of the Wizarding World franchise should look like.

textDisplay: Honestly, if they give Jacob a wand and he can suddenly perform magic that's such an insult to everyone that read the HP books. The lore was well established that Muggle-born witches and wizards and inherently different from muggles. Able to perform magic, extended lives, some degree to see physical incarnations of death, awareness of spectres, etc. Their magic comes not from using a wand, but because they are inherently different. The books established that people died during the Inquisitions because Death Eaters believed Muggle-born witches and wizards stole magic. I'm hoping they're just giving him one so he's less suspect and blends in better. But honestly, seeing how all HP lore has been handled post DH, I wouldn't be surprised.
textOriginal: Honestly, if they give Jacob a wand and he can suddenly perform magic that's such an insult to everyone that read the HP books. The lore was well established that Muggle-born witches and wizards and inherently different from muggles. Able to perform magic, extended lives, some degree to see physical incarnations of death, awareness of spectres, etc. Their magic comes not from using a wand, but because they are inherently different. The books established that people died during the Inquisitions because Death Eaters believed Muggle-born witches and wizards stole magic. I'm hoping they're just giving him one so he's less suspect and blends in better. But honestly, seeing how all HP lore has been handled post DH, I wouldn't be surprised.

textDisplay: I know people hate it cause it's  "different" and "not harry potter", but we're seeing more of the adult side of things from big characters from the HP Universe. In HP we got to see child play with magic and barely anything too advanced...This one is more adults and people who have been out of Hogwarts or whichever wizarding school they were at and have been using magic for more than decade or 2. We get to see an immense level of more advanced magic and use of spells and charms from everyone that we would never get to see in HP because it was still characters that were still learning in school mostly. They hadn't started careers or advanced past Hogwarts yet. Interesting to see new things finally.
textOriginal: I know people hate it cause it's  "different" and "not harry potter", but we're seeing more of the adult side of things from big characters from the HP Universe. In HP we got to see child play with magic and barely anything too advanced...This one is more adults and people who have been out of Hogwarts or whichever wizarding school they were at and have been using magic for more than decade or 2. We get to see an immense level of more advanced magic and use of spells and charms from everyone that we would never get to see in HP because it was still characters that were still learning in school mostly. They hadn't started careers or advanced past Hogwarts yet. Interesting to see new things finally.

textDisplay:
I like your take. I would argue that those films should go even further and not market it to children as much. People of my age who grew up with the books - or, a bit younger, the movies - are now roughly the age of young Dumbledore are Newt Scamander. While I enjoy the casual escapism a diarrhea-christmas-lights fantasy flick with a standard Marvel-level soundtrack gives me, treating these stories of the Harry Potter universe in a more adult way would be a blast to see. At least for the first Fantastic Beasts movie, it was still recognizable as marketed towards a younger audience, while non of the essential charakters were close to that age. That's not requirement, obviously, but it feels ... unfitting? I don't have a proper word for it. 
I hope that this movie will go a little further and not just have "dark" moments for the occassional spookiness but actually explore adult themes a bit deeper.
textOriginal:
I like your take. I would argue that those films should go even further and not market it to children as much. People of my age who grew up with the books - or, a bit younger, the movies - are now roughly the age of young Dumbledore are Newt Scamander. While I enjoy the casual escapism a diarrhea-christmas-lights fantasy flick with a standard Marvel-level soundtrack gives me, treating these stories of the Harry Potter universe in a more adult way would be a blast to see. At least for the first Fantastic Beasts movie, it was still recognizable as marketed towards a younger audience, while non of the essential charakters were close to that age. That's not requirement, obviously, but it feels ... unfitting? I don't have a proper word for it. 
I hope that this movie will go a little further and not just have "dark" moments for the occassional spookiness but actually explore adult themes a bit deeper.

textDisplay:
Idk just trying to mine what I can

The opening quotes are about memory and without it leaving fate to chance

:38 Grindelwald is taking the memory of Yusuf Kama

:52 in the reflection in the bar mirror, that shoulder on the right of the screen, is Yusuf. Also you can see him plenty in the first trailer.

He’s not with the gang in the glass wall hallway place

1:01 he’s standing in the background behind Grindelwald.

He’s not at Hogwarts when everyone else gets a Newt case replica

Curious points:
Everyone has a look alike Newt case at 1:27 and around there.
Also Bunty wasn’t shown walking into the room like the other four, but was shown already in the room, seems odd.

2:07 Who are those people with Yusuf? Perhaps just people following Grindelwald given what Yusuf seems to be doing throughout the trailer.

2:09 Who is that?? This Vicência person on the yellow banner at 2:29 I suppose. Similar to the ones we see at the start going up to Grindelwald’s tower place.

2:38 sitting down in the background, left to right Newt, Yusuf interestingly before he turns or plays a part in the charade, Eulalie, ? In red, ? In green, who are these women?

Cases are meant to hide and trick Grindelwald or Credence. The case is needed to carry what seems to be a baby thestral. What do they need the thestral for? Dumbledore mind hacks, go into another realm or something, and apparently tells Credence the truth Grindelwald is lying about his family. Dumbledore uses the deluminator on/against Credence. Dumbledore and Grindelwald “fight” how much definitely  not the final duel of legend or we only get 3 movies instead of 5. Theseus maybe dies? 

Interesting, interesting.

textDisplay: Like/dislike one's performance is a very subjective thing, but I still feel really weird when I saw comments like "Depp never fitted as Grindelwald anyways." Whether you care about Warner Bros' controversial decision is one thing, but denying Johnny's work is another thing... If you have ever watched the FB2 making-of documentary, you'd realize that Johnny Depp's performance did fit the character well (Personal opinion I think he nailed it and brought it to another level). In the documentary, both JKR and the director mentioned Grindelwald as "rockstar", "seductive," "persuasive," "mysterious." JKR was also the one who insisted on the cast of Johnny Depp back in 2017. So...unless you are telling me that you know Harry Potter/Fantastic Beasts Universe better than JKR herself...?
textOriginal: Like/dislike one's performance is a very subjective thing, but I still feel really weird when I saw comments like "Depp never fitted as Grindelwald anyways." Whether you care about Warner Bros' controversial decision is one thing, but denying Johnny's work is another thing... If you have ever watched the FB2 making-of documentary, you'd realize that Johnny Depp's performance did fit the character well (Personal opinion I think he nailed it and brought it to another level). In the documentary, both JKR and the director mentioned Grindelwald as "rockstar", "seductive," "persuasive," "mysterious." JKR was also the one who insisted on the cast of Johnny Depp back in 2017. So...unless you are telling me that you know Harry Potter/Fantastic Beasts Universe better than JKR herself...?

textDisplay: I'm upset. Mads look nothing like Johnny Depp. This is a plot hole. And Polyjuice Potion can't cover it up because he has no reason to disguise himself.

textDisplay: Exactly. The problem is not Mads and/or who's the better actor of the two because that is subjective. If Mads would've been Grindelwald from the beginning, everyone would've loved it. If it was established from the star that Grindelwald would change the way he looks in every  movie, it's a unique choice, but most people would've been fine with it. The problem is taking Johnny out in the first place after establishing him as the definitive Grindelwald and all the reasons behind and around it. It wasn't a creative choice or anything like that, it was pure bullcrap.
textOriginal: Exactly. The problem is not Mads and/or who's the better actor of the two because that is subjective. If Mads would've been Grindelwald from the beginning, everyone would've loved it. If it was established from the star that Grindelwald would change the way he looks in every  movie, it's a unique choice, but most people would've been fine with it. The problem is taking Johnny out in the first place after establishing him as the definitive Grindelwald and all the reasons behind and around it. It wasn't a creative choice or anything like that, it was pure bullcrap.
