textDisplay:
Main point and questions in this trailer: 
0:05-0:11 What does old Dumbledore see when he’s looking at the pensieve? 
0:21 wow is that McGonagall?!! Btw what is that place? I see a “closed” sign, looks like a store. 
0:35 Is that the symbol of “For The Greater Good”?
0:39-0:42 Dumbledore vs Credence 
0:43-0:49 So originally Jacob didn’t wanna involve🤣 I remember he’s really frightened in the second movie after seeing Grindelwald’s Protego Diabolica. 
1:06-1:08 Dumbledore and Aberforth !!! 
1:06 what are the words behind Aberforth? I saw words like “Do You Know W..... K.....”
1:08 is that McGonagall again?
1:13  Traditional Chinese characters?!!
1:25-1:35 Jacob and Albus are so cute!!! Jacob really trusts Albus. 
1:37-1:38 “OWN KIND” what does it mean? Both are Dumbledores or both are Obscurus? 
1:39 what’s that golden thing?
1:39-1:43 Did Albus kill Credence?😭 
1:16-1:18 1:43-1:45 Grindelwald has been waiting Albus to join him all the time… 
2:06-2:07 Albus vs Grindelwald. Sigh I don’t really like to see this. Two lovers fighting against each other. But Albus is really good at “not caught by surprise” (mentioned in second movie when he’s teaching Mclaggen)  as we can see at 2:06 that he easily dodged to avoid Grindelwald’s spell. Will this be the so called “a shade more skillfull” in fifth movie??? 
2:15 Who’s that hand saving Theasus? Is it Newt?
2:29-2:30 I love this the most!!! Jacob and Albus again. They are so cute><
2:31-2:36 Jacob is doing magic!!! So it’s not a stick but a real wand! Did Albus do some trick on the wand to make it work for Jacob? But wands tend to choose their owners. This wand doesn’t resist Jacob… more importantly it seems he’s doing non verbal magic! Perhaps he has magical ability? In trailer 1 he is sitting with Gryffindor in Hogwarts so does this mean Albus/Hogwarts give him private lessons so he can master certain spells powerfully? 
2:37-2:39 awww Jacob and Albus again! They are too cute. I can’t stop watching them>< Please Albus, give that wand to Jacob. He really loves it besides the wand doesn’t resist him. 
Btw I remember in the second movie Albus once told Newt “Do you know why I admire you? More perhaps than any men I know” Im sure now he has one more beloved one and that is Jacob~ Jacob is just so brave, so cute, so innocent, willing to fight for friends, doesn’t seek power and popularity and only does what it is right. Plus he gets along with Albus so well… looks like he has been his student for a long time. 

This trailer is amazing and im really looking forward to the third movie!!! Hope that there will be one more trailer❤️

textDisplay: Mads Mikkelsen is replacing Johnny Depp, who portrayed Gellert Grindelwald in the previous films. In November 2020, Depp resigned from the role at the request of Warner Bros. due to his personal legal troubles. Later that month, it was reported that Mikkelsen was in early talks to replace Depp as Grindelwald for the 3rd film.
textOriginal: Mads Mikkelsen is replacing Johnny Depp, who portrayed Gellert Grindelwald in the previous films. In November 2020, Depp resigned from the role at the request of Warner Bros. due to his personal legal troubles. Later that month, it was reported that Mikkelsen was in early talks to replace Depp as Grindelwald for the 3rd film.

textDisplay: I remember, it was 2001 when me and my parents went to the cinema to watch the first Harry Potter movie and that day, my childhood memories started to form. In 2016, it was my turn to take my little cousin (8 years old) and introduce him to the wizarding world with the Fantastic Beasts movies and keep this magical universe alive with him creating fond childhood memories. Because memory is everyting...Without it we are blind.
textOriginal: I remember, it was 2001 when me and my parents went to the cinema to watch the first Harry Potter movie and that day, my childhood memories started to form. In 2016, it was my turn to take my little cousin (8 years old) and introduce him to the wizarding world with the Fantastic Beasts movies and keep this magical universe alive with him creating fond childhood memories. Because memory is everyting...Without it we are blind.

textDisplay: This series has its problems, but I personally love how they’ve opened up the magic system. We get to actually SEE what grown wizards are capable of, not just what students at Hogwarts learn. My favorite scene from the original HP films is the Ministry duel between Dumbledore and Voldemort because of the insane magic they use. They’ve done a great job of expanding on that in Fantastic Beasts, and this trailer shows off even more! Very excited!
textOriginal: This series has its problems, but I personally love how they’ve opened up the magic system. We get to actually SEE what grown wizards are capable of, not just what students at Hogwarts learn. My favorite scene from the original HP films is the Ministry duel between Dumbledore and Voldemort because of the insane magic they use. They’ve done a great job of expanding on that in Fantastic Beasts, and this trailer shows off even more! Very excited!

textDisplay:
I'll start by saying this looks amazing but Johnny Depp being cut as Grindelwald is still a sore spot for me. It's unjust and also disconnecting to have such a major role in one of the most popular franchises of all time be recast at what seems to be the end of this specific story. 

With this recast coming near the end of the Fantastic Beasts series I just haven't had enough time to establish a connection Between Grindelwald and Milkensen. When someone says Grindelwald I still immediately think of Depp. I get Dumbledore was recast in original movies but that was due to the OG actor dying. another example is Marvel has had one recast, Hulk, which was at the very beginning of their movie line up so we still had many movies to establish Mark Ruffalo as Hulk.     

I'm glad many people are excited for this movie and I hope it blows the fans away but I'm going to sit out on this one.
textOriginal:

With this recast coming near the end of the Fantastic Beasts series I just haven't had enough time to establish a connection Between Grindelwald and Milkensen. When someone says Grindelwald I still immediately think of Depp. I get Dumbledore was recast in original movies but that was due to the OG actor dying. another example is Marvel has had one recast, Hulk, which was at the very beginning of their movie line up so we still had many movies to establish Mark Ruffalo as Hulk.     

I'm glad many people are excited for this movie and I hope it blows the fans away but I'm going to sit out on this one.

textDisplay:
This looks epic, I hope it doesn't undermine the history HP established like the second one did.  I actually hated the second movie. I am so glad Mads is Grindelwalt now!! 
Unpopular opinion alert! I never liked the casting of Johnny Depp as Grindelwald from the moment it was announced, I have not been a fan of his work of the last few years (nothing to do with his personal troubles). I even preferred  Colin Farrell's performance. I LOVE Mad Mikkelsen as an actor and he fits more with the image I personally had in my head of Grindelwald.
The casting of Jude Law as Dumbledore is just pure brilliance!
textOriginal:
This looks epic, I hope it doesn't undermine the history HP established like the second one did.  I actually hated the second movie. I am so glad Mads is Grindelwalt now!! 
Unpopular opinion alert! I never liked the casting of Johnny Depp as Grindelwald from the moment it was announced, I have not been a fan of his work of the last few years (nothing to do with his personal troubles). I even preferred  Colin Farrell's performance. I LOVE Mad Mikkelsen as an actor and he fits more with the image I personally had in my head of Grindelwald.
The casting of Jude Law as Dumbledore is just pure brilliance!

textDisplay:
I really wish they had separated fantastic beasts into it's own thing. I find combining fantastic beasts with the wizarding war just missed opportunity.  

I think fantastic beasts should have been it's own separate film series that completely focusses on all the fantastic magic beasts found around the world and newts journey studying them.

Then the wizarding war Dumbledore/Grindelwald should have been it's own separate film series.

The plot just feels like a mess where they are just trying to make things fit. Even the title seems odd, the film focus is on the start of the war and the beef between Dumbledor/Grindel but having 'Fantastic beasts' in the name just seems off.
textOriginal:
I really wish they had separated fantastic beasts into it's own thing. I find combining fantastic beasts with the wizarding war just missed opportunity.  

I think fantastic beasts should have been it's own separate film series that completely focusses on all the fantastic magic beasts found around the world and newts journey studying them.

Then the wizarding war Dumbledore/Grindelwald should have been it's own separate film series.

The plot just feels like a mess where they are just trying to make things fit. Even the title seems odd, the film focus is on the start of the war and the beef between Dumbledor/Grindel but having 'Fantastic beasts' in the name just seems off.

textDisplay: Completely agree. I'm especially sad that JD won't be there for the conversation they show. He added so many layers... MM is a great actor, but all I see in the clips shown here is typical rule-the-world, bow-before-me villain. Depp's Grindelwald felt more nuanced. I understand, (because I know someone will come at me for this) that this is only a trailer & there will be more chances for nuance in the film, but this is all I have to go on... & I saw the layers in the last movie's trailer just fine.

textDisplay:
They need to find a new director. David Yates and his desaturated, darkened style is murdering any magic that was left in this franchise. We need a new take. I could accept his style for the later HP movies to match the tone of the series getting darker, but at this point some color would be nice.
On the plus side, seems like Law will continue to kill it as Dumbledore.
textOriginal:
They need to find a new director. David Yates and his desaturated, darkened style is murdering any magic that was left in this franchise. We need a new take. I could accept his style for the later HP movies to match the tone of the series getting darker, but at this point some color would be nice.
On the plus side, seems like Law will continue to kill it as Dumbledore.

textDisplay:
So I'm guessing that these films are part of the movies canon and not the book canon, since so far there have been a lot of inconsistencies with the books, like Dumbledore being the defence against the Dark Arts teacher and not the transfiguration teacher, or McGonagall being a teacher way before she should have even attended Hogwarts, not to mention it can't be a relative of hers since McGonagall is a muggle name that her mother married into. Also Dumbledore knowing about the room of requirement when in the books it's made pretty clear that he'd never discovered it, well he had, but by mistake and didn't know what it was

Maybe after the 5 movies, there will be an explanation. But it being part of the movies canon makes more sense, since McGonagall in the movies is much older than in the books, and a lot of characters backstories aren't given in the films, so I can accept if it's just a different canon
textOriginal:
So I'm guessing that these films are part of the movies canon and not the book canon, since so far there have been a lot of inconsistencies with the books, like Dumbledore being the defence against the Dark Arts teacher and not the transfiguration teacher, or McGonagall being a teacher way before she should have even attended Hogwarts, not to mention it can't be a relative of hers since McGonagall is a muggle name that her mother married into. Also Dumbledore knowing about the room of requirement when in the books it's made pretty clear that he'd never discovered it, well he had, but by mistake and didn't know what it was

Maybe after the 5 movies, there will be an explanation. But it being part of the movies canon makes more sense, since McGonagall in the movies is much older than in the books, and a lot of characters backstories aren't given in the films, so I can accept if it's just a different canon

textDisplay:
Expectation: Johnny Depp
Reality: Mads Mikkelsen

I'm not going to compare one against the other, because I understand that they do have different styles in portraying Grindelwald.

I miss Johnny Depp as Grindelwald, and at the same time, I am also thrilled to see Mads Mikkelsen as Grindelwald. 

We are both fans of the HP & FB movie series, let's not argue about who's the best or not, instead, let's celebrate. 

Blessed Be 🌒🌕🌘

Reality: Mads Mikkelsen

textDisplay:
Even if nothing else came up, I wouldn't recommend Depp.

Depp's energy in most of his films is a bit too close to Helena Bonham Carter as Bellatrix, IMHO. He's not made a career playing subtle masterminds. He can't really do "cold" or "calm" anything so he did "over the top opera villian" rather than "cold evil".
textOriginal:
Even if nothing else came up, I wouldn't recommend Depp.

Depp's energy in most of his films is a bit too close to Helena Bonham Carter as Bellatrix, IMHO. He's not made a career playing subtle masterminds. He can't really do "cold" or "calm" anything so he did "over the top opera villian" rather than "cold evil".

textDisplay:
I agree that the recasting was stupid but that's not exactly fair. An actor was gonna take the vacated spot one way or another. If not Mads Mikkelsen, maybe it would've been Micheal Fassbender, or some other actor that people like. No matter what, this role would've been played by another actor. So why not accept the job when given the opportunity?

Plus it's not like Mads did it out of spite. He actually out-right said in his interviews that he's been a fan of Depp for a long time and that he just took the job cause he liked the character.
textOriginal:
I agree that the recasting was stupid but that's not exactly fair. An actor was gonna take the vacated spot one way or another. If not Mads Mikkelsen, maybe it would've been Micheal Fassbender, or some other actor that people like. No matter what, this role would've been played by another actor. So why not accept the job when given the opportunity?

Plus it's not like Mads did it out of spite. He actually out-right said in his interviews that he's been a fan of Depp for a long time and that he just took the job cause he liked the character.

textDisplay:
So judging from the trailers so far, McGonagall will have more involvement in the series, which kinda gives me hope that she‘ll be more than just fan service and there will be some explanation for her birth date and her appearance in these movies not matching up. Also Jacob is a wizard confirmed!?! There‘s no doubt that spell was cast by him, so either he is magical or Dumbledore enchanted the wand in some form. At least, it‘s definitely not just a random stick that looks like a wand.

Either way, I really like the direction they seem to be going in and I can‘t wait to see this in April once it hits theaters. Hype!!
textOriginal:
So judging from the trailers so far, McGonagall will have more involvement in the series, which kinda gives me hope that she‘ll be more than just fan service and there will be some explanation for her birth date and her appearance in these movies not matching up. Also Jacob is a wizard confirmed!?! There‘s no doubt that spell was cast by him, so either he is magical or Dumbledore enchanted the wand in some form. At least, it‘s definitely not just a random stick that looks like a wand.

Either way, I really like the direction they seem to be going in and I can‘t wait to see this in April once it hits theaters. Hype!!

textDisplay: IMO the majority of the film is fan service. Dumbledore, McGonagall, Hogwarts… One of the many reasons why I enjoyed the first Fantastic Beasts film is because it managed to be its own story, have its own interesting characters. However the second film managed to put paid to that idea with Dumbledore and Hogwarts featuring.
textOriginal: IMO the majority of the film is fan service. Dumbledore, McGonagall, Hogwarts… One of the many reasons why I enjoyed the first Fantastic Beasts film is because it managed to be its own story, have its own interesting characters. However the second film managed to put paid to that idea with Dumbledore and Hogwarts featuring.

textDisplay: Jacob's idea is still stupid. In the books, they clearly stated how much training is neccesary to improve in spellings, it doesn't matter how talented you are, you need practice. ANY 7th grader is a better choice to bring to battle than Jacob and if they use the "Queenie connection" we already got Tina, why couldn't they gone with sisterly love? Jacob works best as a relief character, someone who is in his coffee shop where the heroes go to recover and reunite, not as a combatant.

textDisplay:
My guess is it might tie into the Elder Wand and play into the idea that Dumbledore experimented with creating 'enhanced' wands in his youth. A wand with its own store of magic.

I'm also guessing it's going to backfire on him spectacularly with Grindelwald using it as part of his 'Muggles trying to steal magic' rhetoric. Or possibly as part of Dumbledore's counter narrative with the idea being that Muggles and wizards can live in peace if they can figure out a way to share magic.

Dumbledore and Grindelwald remind me somewhat of the modern depiction on Professor X and Magneto. Former friends turned enemies. This might be equivalent to the idea of awakening the X gene in normal humans. 

I suspect the series will explore the idea of the blurring of lines between wizards and Muggles. Especially if themes of obscureuses being wizards who dangerously repress magic. We might see the idea of Muggles being infused with magic, and I am willing to bet we'll see a Wizard 'burn out' and be rendered Muggle by the end of it. Maybe we'll see Queenie give up her magic entirely. Or perhaps that is how Nagini's transformation becomes permement. Without magic, the curse consumes her.

Either way I think it's interesting that they're going to explore new ground and discuss what actually makes a Wizard a Wizard.
In previous canon wizards and Muggles are clearly different species with muggleborn wizards having a Wizard ancestor somewhere back in the bloodline that sees a reemergence.

I'll be disappointed if it turns out that any muggle can 'learn' magic and there is no innate difference between the two because then it makes the divide even more nonsensical.

I do suspect it is a trick or scheme of some kind amd there will be dome revelation about how it works.

textDisplay: Well I hope the writing for this movie was a lot better. In the previous movie, Depp did his best with such a mediocre material he was given from Rowling, they really underutilized a lot of potential set up for him in that movie. With the writer of the 6 HP movies coming to co-write the script with Rowling, there could be a solid improvement. I can't see this franchise being written only by Rowling because she's not good enough to be a screenwriter yet, just a book author which is completely different
textOriginal: Well I hope the writing for this movie was a lot better. In the previous movie, Depp did his best with such a mediocre material he was given from Rowling, they really underutilized a lot of potential set up for him in that movie. With the writer of the 6 HP movies coming to co-write the script with Rowling, there could be a solid improvement. I can't see this franchise being written only by Rowling because she's not good enough to be a screenwriter yet, just a book author which is completely different
